name: Punalu'u Beach
tagline: A skinny ribbon of sand along the windward coast that locals use for quick dips and fishing — no crowds, ever.
bestTime: Summer mornings; water can be murky after rain
talkStory: Not to be confused with the Big Island's famous black sand beach of the same name, Oahu's Punalu'u ('coral dived for') is a narrow windward strip where local fishermen have cast throw nets for generations. The offshore reef protects a shallow swimming area, but the beach is more about the drive than the destination — the windward coast highway through here is one of the most scenic roads on the island. Ancient Hawaiians cultivated taro in the Punalu'u Valley above, and the area retains a rural, untouched character rare on Oahu.
